data.narrative:
Quobly is a seed-stage quantum computing company founded by Maud Vinet, Tristan Meunier, and François Perruchot. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 observable comparables: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methods are not applicable to Quobly. The quantum computing universe contains only 7 same-sector peers overall, but none satisfy the filtering criteria required for robust triangulation—specifically, no peers exist in the same seed stage, no adjacent-stage cohort reaches the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints, and no similar-valuation-tier clustering emerges. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise given the wide dispersion in quantum technology approaches and commercialization timelines across the comp set.

The absence of sufficiently granular peer data means that valuation must rely on alternative frameworks not present in this dataset—such as DCF sensitivity analysis, venture capital method applied to exit scenarios, or qualitative stage-and-risk assessment benchmarks. Any valuation derived from these public and private quantum comparables should be treated as directional rather than precise, pending either (1) accumulation of more seed-stage quantum companies with disclosed terms, or (2) methodology shifts toward founder experience, IP quality, or capital efficiency metrics that do not depend on peer multiples.
